pages.full_text:
[Hand-drawn route sketch at top showing: mountains, 'Zombai', 'footpath', 'glacier' with arrows indicating direction and elevation]

The glacier appeared to have created a giant elevated causeway on its SE flank as the sun nested that side and forced it to drip boulders and moraine like a huge dam that then kept the glace clinging to the side of the valley.

Simon had lunch at Zamboni in bliding sunlight. Occassionally he glimpsed M. Rosa through clouds towering impossibly above. There were many hangby glaziers high up the summits.

After lunch, he climbed up the side moraine to a cairn where a dutch woman was seated. When he took out his camera she said they had forgotto put the SD card so had no picture of their mountain adventure so Simon took a few of her family and wrote his email address in her iphone so that later she could send her email address and so could send her the photos.
pages.route_sketch_description: Hand-drawn topographic sketch showing mountain terrain with labeled features: 'Zombai', 'footpath', 'glacier', ridge lines, and directional arrows indicating route and elevation changes. Appears to be a schematic of the glacier and surrounding valley geography.
pages.notes: Text contains some unclear readings: 'bliding' (possibly 'blinding'), 'forgotto' (possibly 'forgot to'), 'hangby glaziers' (possibly 'hanging glaciers'), 'glace' (possibly 'glace' or 'glacier'). Route sketch shows topographic detail of glacier terminus and surrounding moraine. Simon appears to be a family member on this mountain hiking trip.

Very twisty roads on the way back to the campsite. We stopped at Bagogna, where there was an old castle built to keep the Swiss away from Lake Maggiore. Then
photo_captions: Mountain landscape - possibly West [?] Sandhertherg or similar caption
pages.notes: Photo caption is faint and difficult to read with certainty. Text is clearly legible but appears to be cut off mid-sentence at bottom of page ('Then'). Narrative describes return journey with stops at historical sites (castle at Bagogna). Swiss/Italian Alps location (Lake Maggiore context). Handwritten page number '22' visible in top right margin.
